justification: The flagged string 'password' is a Swagger UI example value in the API documentation schema definition. It is not a functional credential. The Swagger UI is only accessible in development/staging environments (disabled in production via environment variable). The string is a placeholder for documentation purposes only and is never used in authentication flows.

The SSRF finding flags URL construction in the service discovery module. However, the URL is built exclusively from the Consul service registry (internal DNS only) with a hardcoded scheme (http://) and port. No user input or external data influences the URL. The service registry is on a private network (10.20.0.0/16) with no internet-routable addresses. Verified by reviewing all callers — no path allows external input to reach this method.

The SSRF finding flags URL construction in the partner API client. The base URL is sourced from an allowlisted configuration (partner-endpoints.yaml) that is stored in the GitLab repository and requires merge request approval to modify. The path component is constructed from internal enum values with no user input. URL validation enforces HTTPS scheme and domain must match allowlist. Manual code review confirmed no user-controlled data reaches URL construction. Tested with DAST — no SSRF exploitable.

MD5 is used exclusively for file integrity checksums of non-sensitive report files (PDFs and CSVs) to detect download corruption. It is not used for any security purpose (not passwords, not signatures, not authentication tokens). The context is download verification where collision resistance is not required — only accidental corruption detection. SHA-256 would be functionally equivalent but MD5 is used for legacy compatibility with partner systems that verify against MD5 checksums.
